definition: The fifth letter of the English alphabet, called e and written in the Latin script.
All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items (compare every).
Desirous; keen to do or obtain something.
The state or quality of being eager; ardent desire.
Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
At a time in advance of the usual or expected event.
To gain (success, reward, recognition) through applied effort or work.

Alternative letter-case form of Earth; our planet, third out from the Sun.
Relating to the earth or this world, as opposed to heaven or the heavens.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
A worm that lives in the ground.
Lack of difficulty; the ability to do something easily.
The direction of the earth's rotation, specifically 90°.

A Christian feast commemorating the resurrection of Jesus Christ. It is celebrated on the first Sunday (and Monday) following the full moon that occurs on or next after the vernal equinox, ranging in most of Western Christianity (such as Protestantism and Roman Catholicism) from March 22 to April 25, and in Eastern Christianity (such as the Coptic Church and Eastern Orthodox Church) from April 4 to May 8.
Of, facing, situated in, or related to the east.
The direction or area lying to the east.

An alignment of astronomical objects whereby one object comes between the observer (or notional observer) and another object, thus obscuring the latter.
Relating to ecology, the interrelationships of organisms and their environment.

Moving or operating quickly, as a train not making local stops.
simple past and past participle of express
The action of expressing thoughts, ideas, feelings, etc.
In an express or explicit manner; in a clear or specific manner.
A divided highway, especially one whose intersections and direct access to adjacent properties have been eliminated.
The act of expelling or the state of being expelled.
Especially or extraordinarily fine or pleasing; exceptional.
With inadequate preparation or without advance thought; offhand.
simple past and past participle of extend
The act of extending; a stretching out; enlargement in length, breadth, or time; an increase.
Having a great extent; covering a large area; vast.
A range of values or locations.
Relating to the outside parts or surface of something.
Outside of something; on the exterior.
Archaic spelling of extern (especially in healthcare)
Of fire, etc.: no longer alight; of a light, etc.: no longer shining; extinguished, quenched.
The action of making or becoming extinct; annihilation.
To stop (fire, etc.) from burning; also, to stop (light, etc.) from shining; to put out, to quench.
Beyond what is due, usual, expected, or necessary; extraneous; additional; supernumerary.
Something that is extracted or drawn out.
The act of extracting or the condition of being extracted.
To remove a person from one state to another by legal process.

A formal process by which a criminal suspect held by one government or jurisdiction is handed over to another government or jurisdiction for trial or, if the suspect has already been tried and found guilty, to serve his or her sentence.
Excessive or superfluous expenditure of money.
Exceeding the bounds of something; roving; hence, foreign.
Of a place, the most remote, farthest or outermost.
A person who holds extreme views, especially one who advocates such views; a radical or fanatic.
To free, disengage, loosen, or untangle.
An extroverted person: one who is outgoing, sociable, and concerned with outer affairs.
Very cheery and peppy; extremely cheerful, energetic and enthusiastic.
To discharge through pores or incisions, as moisture or other liquid matter; to give out.
To rejoice; to be very happy, especially in triumph; to triumph (over).
An organ through which animals see (“perceive surroundings via light”).
The hair that grows over the bone ridge above the eye socket.
One of the hairs which grows along the edge of eyelids.
A thin skin membrane that covers and moves over an eye.
Vision or the faculty of sight.
Someone who sees an event and can report or testify about it.
